title: PoC scope is full stack: broker + MCP wrapper + agent configuration + deploy
content: The agent-messaging-service PoC ships the entire vertical slice in one pass — Cloudflare Worker with the ConversationDO and SessionDO classes, the six-tool MCP wrapper at /mcp, the magic-link control plane, the deployed instance, and the two-Claude-Code agent configuration that satisfies SPEC.md §3.2. Half-measures (broker only, or agents-against-mock-broker) are explicitly rejected — POC-PLAN.md already commits to the demo gate as the only success measure, and partial scope cannot validate the demo gate. The "build and test" framing covers all of it.

title: Two AI tools are MCP-configured Claude Code instances; no programmatic harness for v1
content: The "two AI tools that talk" are two Claude Code instances, each configured with the AMS MCP server in .mcp.json under separate AMS account bearers. There is no parallel programmatic agent harness using the Anthropic SDK directly in v1. Rationale: SPEC.md §3.2 demo gate is "two real Claude Code instances on two physical machines"; a programmatic harness validates the wire and wrapper but not the demo gate, and maintaining both doubles surface area for the same evidence. The MCP wrapper is the abstraction; Claude Code is the runtime; the operator-side artifact is two .mcp.json files plus the prompts. Test pyramid still includes unit + wire integration + MCP wrapper integration with a mock peer (the lower three rungs); the "programmatic E2E" rung is dropped, "live demo gate" is the only end-to-end.

title: Multi-host CNAME deployment: one Worker behind ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai
content: AMS deploys as a single Cloudflare Worker fronted by multiple custom domains via CNAME, Sovee-pattern. v1 launches with two: ams.klappy.dev and ams.truthkit.ai. Both CNAMEs route to the same Worker, the same KV namespace, the same DO classes. Magic links are host-portable: a link minted under ams.klappy.dev and opened against ams.truthkit.ai resolves the same conversation because the Worker is the same. Account namespaces are global (a klappy account works under either host). The host carries no semantic distinction at the wire or protocol layer — it is brand surface and tenant-affinity for vanity routing. Sovee precedent: at Sovee, CNAMEs as the multi-tenant abstraction enabled per-customer branding without per-customer infrastructure. Same play here at the broker layer. Reversibility: one-way for v1 — splitting into per-host Workers would break magic-link portability. Designated D0011 by operator confirmation 2026-05-02. Filename: canon/decisions/D0011-multi-host-cname-deployment.md.

title: Validation uses a fresh Claude session with the journal as the handoff surface
content: Per klappy://canon/principles/verification-requires-fresh-context and the operating contract, validation of the PoC ships requires structural separation from the executing session. The executing Claude writes evidence into journal/ as it goes — smoke test outputs, demo run logs, deviation notes, screenshots — as a self-contained record. A fresh Claude session opens cold, reads the journal entry, validates against SPEC.md §3.1 (smoke test) and §3.2 (demo gate), and produces a findings entry with explicit accept | iterate | pivot disposition. Same model family is acceptable; same session is not. The journal must be readable by the fresh session without any in-context memory from the build.

title: SPEC.md and the deeper docs are updated before any code lands, per D0007 and SPEC §14
content: The hostname change from ams.covenant.dev to the dual-host pattern is load-bearing per the SPEC §3.1 smoke test, §3.2 demo gate, §6 architecture diagram, §11 open decisions, and the wrangler.toml routes block in POC-INFRA §8.1. Per D0007 (SPEC.md is the locking surface) and SPEC §14 (revision discipline: spec updates first, deeper docs aligned), the doc updates land in a single PR before any worker/src/ files are written. Files affected: SPEC.md, POC-INFRA.md, POC-PLAN.md, PROTOCOL.md, AMS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ESSAY.md, README.md, canon/decisions/D0010-observability-via-subscriber-not-wire.md (one passing reference). New file: canon/decisions/D{NNNN}-multi-host-cname-deployment.md. Skipping the spec-first step would let code diverge from the locking surface, which is exactly the failure mode D0007 was written to prevent.

title: The hostname ams.covenant.dev appears in 10+ repo files but does not exist as a deploy target
content: Grep across the repo finds 18 references to ams.covenant.dev across SPEC.md, POC-INFRA.md, POC-PLAN.md, PROTOCOL.md, AMS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ESSAY.md, README.md, and canon/decisions/D0010. The hostname was introduced by a prior session and was not corrected before this planning session. The README also contains "Built under Covenant Venture Studio" which describes the org behind AMS — that reference is correct and stays; only the deploy hostname is wrong. Origin: prior Claude session conflated the org-of-record with a plausible deploy domain.

title: Sovee CNAME pattern is the operational counterpart to per-query dynamic orchestration
content: The Sovee inversion already lives in canon as the design forcing function (ams://canon/principles/per-query-dynamic-orchestration): index globally, compose per-query, beat pre-trained capability with just-in-time work. The CNAME deployment pattern is the same inversion at the infrastructure layer: one engine, many domains, multi-tenant via DNS rather than via per-tenant deployment. The lineage is consistent — at Sovee, CNAMEs let the same translation engine serve N branded customer surfaces. At AMS, CNAMEs let one broker serve N organizational surfaces. The forcing function — keep the per-hop substrate cheap so dynamic composition stays cheaper than declaration — is preserved by single-Worker multi-host because it does not add per-host setup cost.

title: Next executable step: spec-update PR, awaiting operator go
content: The next action when execution starts is a single PR that (1) replaces all 18 ams.covenant.dev references with the dual-host pattern across SPEC.md, POC-INFRA.md, POC-PLAN.md, PROTOCOL.md, AMS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ESSAY.md, README.md, and canon/decisions/D0010, (2) adds a new canon decision file at canon/decisions/D{NNNN}-multi-host-cname-deployment.md encoding the constraint above, (3) updates POC-INFRA §8 wrangler.toml to declare both custom-domain routes, (4) updates SPEC §14 revisions table with the entry. After merge, POC-PLAN Day 1 (Worker scaffold + accounts endpoint) becomes the first code-producing step. Operator must say go before this PR is opened. The branch name proposal: spec/dual-host-deploy.

title: D0011 numbering resolved: multi-host CNAME takes D0011; success criteria stays journal-only
content: The journal entry 2026-05-02-ams-sovee-substrate-principle.tsv flagged D0011 as a candidate designation for "AMS success criteria locked: utility-to-self plus maintainable foundation." This planning session also produced a candidate worth a D-number (the multi-host CNAME deployment). Operator (klappy) confirmed 2026-05-02 that D0011 = multi-host CNAME deployment. Rationale: D0001–D0010 are all "how the system works" (wire, ownership, addressing, architecture, governance); multi-host CNAME is "how the system deploys," same category as D0006 (dream-house wire) and D0010 (observability subscriber). Success criteria is values-class, not protocol-class — stays as the journal entry it already is, possibly later promoted to canon/values/success-criteria.md without a D-number.
